title: Archive: LEAP-Hand Glove / Data-Capture Glove
description: Archive reason: DexUMI and Sunday Robotics' data-capture glove stacks already cover the intended idea: collect dexterous human-hand demonstrations through a wearable glove/exoskeleton, preserve haptic/contact cues, and translate those demonstrations into robot-hand policy data.
summary: The LEAP-Hand glove idea is archived because the desired data-collection shape has effectively been done. DexUMI uses the human hand as a universal manipulation interface with a wearable exoskeleton, direct haptic feedback, tactile sensing, visual adaptation, and robot-hand policy learning. Sunday Robotics independently validates the same product-scale thesis with a Skill/Memory/Data Capture Glove plus Skill Transform pipeline for translating human glove data into robot data. Keep this card as a reference, not a standalone project.

details.text: DexUMI directly covers the academic version of the idea: a data collection and policy learning framework that uses the human hand as the manipulation interface, bridges the kinematic gap with a wearable hand exoskeleton, preserves direct haptic feedback during collection, adds tactile sensing, adapts human motion to feasible robot-hand motion, and uses visual inpainting to reduce the human/robot embodiment gap.

Sunday Robotics covers the product/data-engine version: their Skill Capture Glove / Memory Glove captures human task data through force sensors, cameras, and joint potentiometers; the glove and robot hand are co-designed to share geometry and sensor layout; Skill Transform converts glove demonstrations into robot-like kinematic and visual training data.

details: Earlier LEAP Hand / GET-Zero-style hardware references remain useful for embodiment and low-cost hand design, but they are not the real archive reason for this card. The reason is that DexUMI and Sunday already demonstrate the glove-based dexterous data capture route the original idea wanted.

Moved-out live work: VTLA should keep unresolved tactile/contact representation questions, Real Robot Infra should own Wuji/MANUS glove integration and wrappers, and Open-Hardware should only score concrete buy/build decisions.

Archive decision: do not keep a separate LEAP-hand glove project unless we are building a distinct lab-specific data-capture glove that is meaningfully different from DexUMI/Sunday and tied to a named hardware owner, dataset schema, and validation task.
risks_decisions: Archived 2026-05-31 after per-project survey audit; archive reason corrected 2026-06-05 per user: DexUMI and Sunday Robotics data-capture gloves already complete the intended direction.
Reason type: externally completed glove-based dexterous data collection and human-to-robot demonstration translation route.
Reopen condition: a lab-specific glove/exoskeleton design has a distinct sensor stack, robot-hand mapping, dataset schema, and validation task not covered by DexUMI or Sunday.
